op_000001.proof_sketch: Refute the universal equivalence by taking A = Tp.atom "p" and B = (Tp.atom "q" ‚ß∏ Tp.atom "p") ‚ßπ Tp.atom "q". There is an explicit derivation [A] ‚áí B: first derive [(Tp.atom "q" ‚ß∏ Tp.atom "p"), Tp.atom "p"] ‚áí Tp.atom "q" with Sequent.rdiv_l from two axioms, then apply Sequent.ldiv_r. If the target statement were true, this derivation would force A = B, but after unfolding A and B this is an impossible equality between different Tp constructors.

intro h
let A : Tp := Tp.atom "p"
let B : Tp := (Tp.atom "q" ‚ß∏ Tp.atom "p") ‚ßπ Tp.atom "q"
have hseq : [A] ‚áí B := by
  dsimp [A, B]
  apply Sequent.ldiv_r
  ¬∑ simp
  ¬∑ simpa using
      (Sequent.rdiv_l
        (Œî := [Tp.atom "p"])
        (A := Tp.atom "p")
        (Œì := [])
        (B := Tp.atom "q")
        (Œõ := [])
        (C := Tp.atom "q")
        Sequent.ax
        Sequent.ax)
have hEq : A = B := (h (A := A) (B := B)).mp hseq
dsimp [A, B] at hEq
cases hEq

op_000002.proof_sketch: Instantiate the statement with Œì = [#"p"] and A = ((#"q" ‚ß∏ #"p") ‚ßπ #"q"). The context assumption is immediate because the only member of Œì is atomic. Derive [#"q" ‚ß∏ #"p", #"p"] ‚áí #"q" using `Sequent.rdiv_l` from the two axioms `[ #"p" ] ‚áí #"p"` and `[ #"q" ] ‚áí #"q"`, then derive [#"p"] ‚áí ((#"q" ‚ß∏ #"p") ‚ßπ #"q") using `Sequent.ldiv_r`. Applying the claimed statement yields `is_atom ((#"q" ‚ß∏ #"p") ‚ßπ #"q")`, which simplifies to `False`.

intro h
let p : Tp := #"p"
let q : Tp := #"q"
let A : Tp := ((q ‚ß∏ p) ‚ßπ q)
have hctx : ‚àÄ x ‚àà [p], is_atom x := by
  intro x hx
  simp at hx
  subst hx
  simp [p, is_atom]
have hpq : [q ‚ß∏ p, p] ‚áí q := by
  have hp : [p] ‚áí p := Sequent.ax
  have hq : [q] ‚áí q := Sequent.ax
  simpa using
    (Sequent.rdiv_l (Œì := []) (Œî := [p]) (Œõ := []) (A := p) (B := q) (C := q) hp hq)
have hder : [p] ‚áí A := by
  have hne : [p] ‚âÝ [] := by simp
  simpa [A] using (Sequent.ldiv_r (Œì := [p]) (A := q ‚ß∏ p) (B := q) hne hpq)
have hatom : is_atom A := (h hctx hder).1
simpa [A, is_atom] using hatom

op_000008.proof_sketch: Use proof-search completeness on the forward implication. For a singleton context, `prove1 [A] (Tp.atom s)` simplifies by cases on `A`: if `A` is atomic, it forces the singleton-equality branch; if `A` is a slash type, the only candidate branch requires `prove1 [] X`, but `prove1 [] X = false` for every `X`, so those cases are impossible. The reverse implication is just `Sequent.ax` after rewriting by the equality.
op_000008.proof_text:
intro A s
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  have hnil : ‚àÄ X : Tp, prove1 [] X = false := by
    intro X
    cases X <;> simp [prove1, candidates, picks, splits]
  have hp : prove1 [A] (Tp.atom s) := prove1_complete h
  cases A with
  | atom t =>
      simp [prove1, candidates, picks, splits] at hp
      cases hp
      rfl
  | ldiv A B =>
      simp [prove1, candidates, picks, splits, hnil] at hp
  | rdiv B A =>
      simp [prove1, candidates, picks, splits, hnil] at hp
¬∑ intro h
  simpa [h] using (Sequent.ax : [Tp.atom s] ‚áí Tp.atom s)

op_000008.proof_sketch: Keep the previous proof-search strategy, but avoid `simp [prove1, ...]` because the recursive equation theorem for `prove1` loops. First prove `prove1 [] X = false` by `unfold prove1` and case split on `X`. Then convert the sequent hypothesis to `prove1 [A] (Tp.atom s)` using completeness, case split on `A`, and unfold `prove1` only once in each branch. The atomic branch simplifies to the singleton-equality case; the slash branches simplify to contradiction because they require the empty-context subcall. The reverse implication is `Sequent.ax` after rewriting by the equality.
op_000008.proof_text:
intro A s
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  have hnil : ‚àÄ X : Tp, prove1 [] X = false := by
    intro X
    unfold prove1
    cases X <;> simp [candidates, picks]
  have hp : prove1 [A] (Tp.atom s) := prove1_complete h
  cases A with
  | atom t =>
      unfold prove1 at hp
      simp [candidates, picks] at hp
      cases hp
      rfl
  | ldiv A B =>
      unfold prove1 at hp
      simp [candidates, picks, splits, hnil] at hp
  | rdiv B A =>
      unfold prove1 at hp
      simp [candidates, picks, splits, hnil] at hp
¬∑ intro h
  simpa [h] using (Sequent.ax : [Tp.atom s] ‚áí Tp.atom s)

intro A B
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  cases B with
  | atom s =>
      exact Or.inl (thm_singleton_atom_iff_eq_000008.mp h)
  | rdiv D C =>
      exact Or.inr <| Or.inl ‚ü®C, D, rfl, rdiv_invertible h‚ü©
  | ldiv C D =>
      exact Or.inr <| Or.inr ‚ü®C, D, rfl, ldiv_invertible h‚ü©
¬∑ intro h

  ¬∑ cases hEq

  ¬∑ exact Sequent.ldiv_r (Œì := [A]) (A := C) (B := D) (by simp) hCD

op_000021.source_statement: For atomic goals, successful runs of `proveAux` are in height-preserving correspondence with focused decomposition trees: `proveAux n Œì (Tp.atom s) = true` exactly when there exists a focused atomic decomposition tree for `Œì ‚áí Tp.atom s` of height at most `n`.
op_000021.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ {n : Nat} {Œì : List Tp} {s : String}, proveAux n Œì (# s) = true ‚Üî FocusedTree n Œì (# s)
op_000021.statement_formalization_notes: No decomposition-tree predicate appears in the provided context, so the prelude introduces a small recursive Prop `FocusedTree` that conservatively mirrors the branching structure of `proveAux`; its fuel parameter is used as the height-at-most bound.

op_000021.proof_sketch: Strengthen to all succedents `A` and prove by induction on `n`. After splitting on `A`, `proveAux` and `FocusedTree` have matching recursion equations; the only non-definitional steps are converting `decide (...) = true` into propositions and case-splitting on the candidate witness in the atomic case.
op_000021.prelude_code:
def FocusedTree : Nat ‚Üí List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | 0, _, _ => False
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.atom s =>
      Œì = [Tp.atom s] ‚à®
      ‚àÉ c ‚àà candidates Œì,
        match c with
        | Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ =>
            FocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß FocusedTree n (L ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s)
        | Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R =>
            FocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß FocusedTree n (Œì‚ÇÅ ++ [B] ++ R) (Tp.atom s)
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.ldiv A B =>
      Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚àß FocusedTree n ([A] ++ Œì) B
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.rdiv B A =>
      Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚àß FocusedTree n (Œì ++ [A]) B
op_000021.proof_text:
suffices hmain : ‚àÄ {n : Nat} {Œì : List Tp} {A : Tp}, proveAux n Œì A = true ‚Üî FocusedTree n Œì A
¬∑ intro n Œì s
  simpa using (hmain (n := n) (Œì := Œì) (A := # s))
¬∑ intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      intro Œì A
      cases A <;> simp [proveAux, FocusedTree]
  | succ n ih =>
      intro Œì A
      cases A with
      | atom s =>
          simp only [proveAux, FocusedTree, Bool.or_eq_true, decide_eq_true_eq, List.any_eq_true]
          constructor
          ¬∑ intro h
            rcases h with h | ‚ü®c, hc, hcTree‚ü©
            ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
            ¬∑ refine Or.inr ‚ü®c, hc, ?_‚ü©
              cases c <;> simpa [Bool.and_eq_true, ih] using hcTree
          ¬∑ intro h
            rcases h with h | ‚ü®c, hc, hcTree‚ü©
            ¬∑ exact Or.inl h
            ¬∑ refine Or.inr ‚ü®c, hc, ?_‚ü©
              cases c <;> simpa [Bool.and_eq_true, ih] using hcTree
      | ldiv A B =>
          simp [proveAux, FocusedTree, Bool.and_eq_true, ih]
      | rdiv B A =>
          simp [proveAux, FocusedTree, Bool.and_eq_true, ih]

op_000011.proof_sketch: The proposed induction breaks in the slash-right cases: an atomic context can derive a non-atomic succedent because the premise may add a non-atomic formula and then use left rules there. A concrete two-atom context gives a counterexample.
op_000011.counterexample_text: Take Œì = [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p"] and A = Tp.rdiv (Tp.atom "q") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q"))). Then every x ‚àà Œì is atomic. Also Œì ‚áí A: first, [Tp.atom "r", Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q")] ‚áí Tp.atom "q" by Sequent.ldiv_l from the axioms [Tp.atom "r"] ‚áí Tp.atom "r" and [Tp.atom "q"] ‚áí Tp.atom "q". Next, [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p", Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q"))] ‚áí Tp.atom "q" by another Sequent.ldiv_l, using [Tp.atom "p"] ‚áí Tp.atom "p" and the previous derivation as the main premise. Since [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p"] ‚âÝ [], Sequent.rdiv_r yields [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p"] ‚áí A. But Œì is not equal to [Tp.atom s] for any string s, since it has two entries. Therefore the statement is false.

op_000011.proof_sketch: Instantiate the universal claim with Œì = [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p"] and A = Tp.rdiv (Tp.atom "q") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q"))). The context is atomic by inspection. Build a derivation of Œì ‚áí A by first deriving [Tp.atom "r", Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q")] ‚áí Tp.atom "q" via Sequent.ldiv_l from two axioms, then deriving [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p", Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "p") (Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q"))] ‚áí Tp.atom "q" by a second Sequent.ldiv_l, and finally applying Sequent.rdiv_r. The claimed conclusion would give [Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p"] = [Tp.atom s] for some s, contradicting equality of list lengths.
op_000011.proof_text:
intro h
have h_atoms : ‚àÄ x ‚àà ([Tp.atom "r", Tp.atom "p"] : List Tp), is_atom x := by
  intro x hx
  simp at hx
  rcases hx with rfl | rfl <;> simp [is_atom]
have h_rq : [Tp.atom "r", Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q")] ‚áí Tp.atom "q" := by
  simpa using
    (Sequent.ldiv_l

op_000025.source_statement: There exist Œì and A such that every member of Œì is atomic, Œì ‚áí A is derivable in the full product-free calculus, but the corresponding sequent is not derivable in either the left-only fragment or the right-only fragment.

    Left.ctxToProductFree Œì' = Œì ‚àß A'.toProductFree = A ‚àß Left.Sequent Œì' A'

constructor

        (Œî := [Tp.atom "r"]) (A := Tp.atom "r")

        (Œî := [Tp.atom "p"]) (A := Tp.atom "p")
        (Œì := [Tp.atom "r"]) (B := Tp.ldiv (Tp.atom "r") (Tp.atom "q")) (Œõ := []) (C := Tp.atom "q")

  ¬∑ simpa using h_rpq
constructor
¬∑ intro hLeft

¬∑ intro hRight

op_000029.source_statement: ‚àÄ N : Nat, ‚àÉ Œì : List Tp, ‚àÉ A : Tp, list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = N ‚àß Œì ‚áí A ‚àß FocusedTree (N + 1) Œì A ‚àß ‚àÄ m < N + 1, ¬¨ FocusedTree m Œì A
op_000029.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ N : Nat, ‚àÉ Œì : List Tp, ‚àÉ A : Tp, list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = N ‚àß Œì ‚áí A ‚àß FocusedTree (N + 1) Œì A ‚àß ‚àÄ m < N + 1, ¬¨ FocusedTree m Œì A
op_000029.statement_formalization_notes: Used the statement verbatim with repository notation for sequents, degrees, and FocusedTree.

op_000029.proof_sketch: Refute the universal statement by specializing to N = 0. Any witness would give Œì and A with list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = 0 and Œì ‚áí A. But Œì ‚áí A implies Œì is nonempty by nonempty_premises, hence list_degree Œì > 0; also every type has tp_degree A > 0 by case analysis on A. Therefore the sum is positive, contradicting the required equality to 0. The FocusedTree conjuncts are irrelevant.
op_000029.proof_text:
intro hforall
rcases hforall 0 with ‚ü®Œì, A, hdeg, hder, _, _‚ü©
have hApos : 0 < tp_degree A := by
  cases A <;> simp [tp_degree]
have hŒìpos : 0 < list_degree Œì := by
  rcases List.exists_cons_of_ne_nil (nonempty_premises hder) with ‚ü®B, Œî, rfl‚ü©
  have hBpos : 0 < tp_degree B := by
    cases B <;> simp [tp_degree]
  simpa [list_degree] using add_pos_of_pos_of_nonneg hBpos (Nat.zero_le (list_degree Œî))
have hsum : 0 < list_degree Œì + tp_degree A := add_pos hŒìpos hApos
exact (Nat.ne_of_gt hsum) hdeg
op_000029.counterexample_text: Take N = 0. If the statement were true, there would be Œì and A with list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = 0 and Œì ‚áí A. But derivability forces Œì ‚âÝ [] by nonempty_premises, so list_degree Œì > 0, and every Tp has positive degree, so tp_degree A > 0. Hence the sum is strictly positive, contradiction.

op_000033.source_statement: ‚àÄ N : Nat, 2 ‚â§ N ‚Üí ‚àÉ Œì : List Tp, ‚àÉ A : Tp, list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = N ‚àß Œì ‚áí A ‚àß FocusedTree (N + 1) Œì A ‚àß ‚àÄ m < N + 1, ¬¨ FocusedTree m Œì A
op_000033.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ N : Nat, 2 ‚â§ N ‚Üí ‚àÉ Œì : List Tp, ‚àÉ A : Tp, list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = N ‚àß Œì ‚áí A ‚àß FocusedTree (N + 1) Œì A ‚àß ‚àÄ m < N + 1, ¬¨ FocusedTree m Œì A
op_000033.statement_formalization_notes: Used the statement verbatim with the repository‚Äôs existing notation for sequents, degrees, and `FocusedTree`; no prelude declarations are needed.

op_000033.proof_sketch: Instantiate the statement at N = 2. For any witness Œì, A with list_degree Œì + tp_degree A = 2 and Œì ‚áí A, derivability forces Œì to be nonempty, hence both summands are positive and must therefore each equal 1. So A is an atom, and Œì has total degree 1. Since Œì is nonempty, that forces Œì to be a singleton [B], and the head B also has degree 1, hence is atomic. Then the singleton derivation [B] ‚áí #s implies B = #s, so Œì = [#s] and A = #s. But then FocusedTree 1 [#s] (#s) holds by the atomic base clause, contradicting the required condition that every m < 3 fails.

intro hforall
rcases hforall 2 (by simp) with ‚ü®Œì, A, hdeg, hder, _, hmin‚ü©
have hApos : 0 < tp_degree A := by
  cases A <;> simp [tp_degree]
have hŒìpos : 0 < list_degree Œì := by
  rcases List.exists_cons_of_ne_nil (nonempty_premises hder) with ‚ü®B, Œî, rfl‚ü©
  have hBpos : 0 < tp_degree B := by
    cases B <;> simp [tp_degree]
  simpa [list_degree] using add_pos_of_pos_of_nonneg hBpos (Nat.zero_le (list_degree Œî))
have hAone : tp_degree A = 1 := by
  omega
have hŒìone : list_degree Œì = 1 := by
  omega
cases A with
| atom s =>
    rcases List.exists_cons_of_ne_nil (nonempty_premises hder) with ‚ü®B, Œî, hŒìcons‚ü©
    subst Œì
    have hŒînil : Œî = [] := by
      cases Œî with
      | nil => rfl
      | cons C Œò =>
          exfalso
          have hBpos : 0 < tp_degree B := by
            cases B <;> simp [tp_degree]
          have hTailPos : 0 < list_degree (C :: Œò) := by
            have hCpos : 0 < tp_degree C := by
              cases C <;> simp [tp_degree]
            simpa [list_degree] using add_pos_of_pos_of_nonneg hCpos (Nat.zero_le (list_degree Œò))
          have : tp_degree B + list_degree (C :: Œò) = 1 := by
            simpa [list_degree] using hŒìone
          omega
    rw [hŒînil] at hŒìone hder hmin
    have hBone : tp_degree B = 1 := by
      simpa [list_degree] using hŒìone
    cases B with
    | atom t =>
        have hBt : Tp.atom t = Tp.atom s := by
          exact thm_singleton_atom_iff_eq_000008.mp hder
        have hft1 : FocusedTree 1 [Tp.atom s] (Tp.atom s) := by
          simp [FocusedTree]
        exact (hmin 1 (by simp)) (by simpa [hBt] using hft1)
    | ldiv B1 B2 =>
        exfalso
        have hBcontra := hBone
        simp [tp_degree] at hBcontra
        have hB1pos : 0 < tp_degree B1 := by
          cases B1 <;> simp [tp_degree]
        omega
    | rdiv B1 B2 =>
        exfalso
        have hBcontra := hBone
        simp [tp_degree] at hBcontra
        have hB1pos : 0 < tp_degree B1 := by
          cases B1 <;> simp [tp_degree]
        omega
| ldiv A1 A2 =>
    exfalso
    have hAcontra := hAone
    simp [tp_degree] at hAcontra
    have hA1pos : 0 < tp_degree A1 := by
      cases A1 <;> simp [tp_degree]
    omega
| rdiv A1 A2 =>
    exfalso
    have hAcontra := hAone
    simp [tp_degree] at hAcontra
    have hA1pos : 0 < tp_degree A1 := by
      cases A1 <;> simp [tp_degree]
    omega

Hence A is atomic and Œì is a singleton context [B] with B also atomic. The singleton derivation [B] ‚áí A then forces B = A, so the only possible witness is Œì = [#s], A = #s. But FocusedTree 1 [#s] (#s) holds immediately, contradicting the requirement that every m < 3 fail.

  rcases h with hEq | ‚ü®c, hc, hcProof‚ü©
  ¬∑ exact Or.inl hEq

def LeftDivisionOnly : Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Tp.atom _ => True
  | Tp.ldiv A B => LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B
  | Tp.rdiv _ _ => False

def RightDivisionOnly : Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | Tp.atom _ => True
  | Tp.ldiv _ _ => False
  | Tp.rdiv A B => RightDivisionOnly A ‚àß RightDivisionOnly B

abbrev SameDivisionOrientation (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  LeftDivisionOnly A ‚à® RightDivisionOnly A

lemma left_only_of_left_tp (A : Left.Tp) : LeftDivisionOnly A.toProductFree := by
  induction A with

  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>

lemma right_only_of_right_tp (A : Right.Tp) : RightDivisionOnly A.toProductFree := by
  induction A with

lemma exists_left_tp_of_left_only {A : Tp} (hA : LeftDivisionOnly A) :
    ‚àÉ A' : Left.Tp, A'.toProductFree = A := by
  induction A with

  | ldiv A B ihA ihB =>

      rcases ihB hB with ‚ü®B', rfl‚ü©
      exact ‚ü®Left.Tp.ldiv A' B', rfl‚ü©
  | rdiv A B =>

lemma exists_right_tp_of_right_only {A : Tp} (hA : RightDivisionOnly A) :

  | rdiv B A ihB ihA =>
      rcases hA with ‚ü®hB, hA‚ü©
      rcases ihB hB with ‚ü®B', rfl‚ü©

op_000050.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì Œî Œõ : List Tp) (A B : Tp), ((LeftRep Œì A ‚àß LeftRep (Œî ++ [A] ++ Œõ) B) ‚Üí LeftRep (Œî ++ Œì ++ Œõ) B) ‚àß ((RightRep Œì A ‚àß RightRep (Œî ++ [A] ++ Œõ) B) ‚Üí RightRep (Œî ++ Œì ++ Œõ) B)
op_000050.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the two clauses as a single universally quantified conjunction, using the existing `LeftRep` and `RightRep` abbreviations from `Derived.lean`.

op_000050.proof_sketch: Convert each `LeftRep`/`RightRep` hypothesis to orientation plus derivability via the exactness theorems `thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058` and `thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059`. Use `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible` on the derivations, then combine the orientation assumptions over `Œî`, `Œì`, and `Œõ` and repackage with `thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055`.
op_000050.proof_text:
intro Œì Œî Œõ A B
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with ‚ü®hŒìA, hŒîAŒõB‚ü©
  have hLeft1 := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 Œì A).mp hŒìA
  have hLeft2 := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 (Œî ++ [A] ++ Œõ) B).mp hŒîAŒõB
  apply (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 (Œî ++ Œì ++ Œõ) B).1
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro y hy
    have hy' : y = B ‚à® y ‚àà Œî ‚à® y ‚àà Œì ‚à® y ‚àà Œõ := by
      simpa [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, or_assoc, or_left_comm, or_comm] using hy
    rcases hy' with rfl | hyŒî | hyŒì | hyŒõ
    ¬∑ exact hLeft2.1 y (by simp)
    ¬∑ exact hLeft2.1 y (by simp [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, hyŒî])
    ¬∑ exact hLeft1.1 y (by simp [List.mem_cons, hyŒì])
    ¬∑ exact hLeft2.1 y (by simp [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, hyŒõ])
  ¬∑ exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible hLeft1.2 hLeft2.2
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with ‚ü®hŒìA, hŒîAŒõB‚ü©
  have hRight1 := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 Œì A).mp hŒìA
  have hRight2 := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 (Œî ++ [A] ++ Œõ) B).mp hŒîAŒõB
  apply (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 (Œî ++ Œì ++ Œõ) B).2
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro y hy
    have hy' : y = B ‚à® y ‚àà Œî ‚à® y ‚àà Œì ‚à® y ‚àà Œõ := by
      simpa [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, or_assoc, or_left_comm, or_comm] using hy
    rcases hy' with rfl | hyŒî | hyŒì | hyŒõ
    ¬∑ exact hRight2.1 y (by simp)
    ¬∑ exact hRight2.1 y (by simp [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, hyŒî])
    ¬∑ exact hRight1.1 y (by simp [List.mem_cons, hyŒì])
    ¬∑ exact hRight2.1 y (by simp [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, hyŒõ])
  ¬∑ exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.cut_admissible hRight1.2 hRight2.2

op_000062.source_statement: For every context Œì and type A, if every formula in A :: Œì is LeftDivisionOnly and Œì ‚áí A, then there exists a focused proof tree for Œì ‚áí A such that every node Œî ‚áí B in the tree also satisfies ‚àÄ x ‚àà B :: Œî, LeftDivisionOnly x.
op_000062.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), ((LeftOnlyNode Œì A) ‚àß Œì ‚áí A) ‚Üí ‚àÉ n : Nat, LeftFocusedTree n Œì A
op_000062.statement_formalization_notes: Introduced `LeftFocusedTree` as a recursive strengthening of `FocusedTree` that records the `LeftDivisionOnly` condition at every node.

op_000062.proof_sketch: Use `thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023` to obtain some `n` with `FocusedTree n Œì A` from the sequent hypothesis. It then suffices to prove by induction on `n` that `LeftOnlyNode Œì A` upgrades any `FocusedTree n Œì A` to `LeftFocusedTree n Œì A`; in the atomic case, an `rdiv` candidate contradicts `LeftDivisionOnly`, while an `ldiv` candidate propagates left-onlyness to both recursive subtrees via `candidates_list_degree` and the left-onlyness of the principal `A ‚ßπ B`.
op_000062.prelude_code:
abbrev LeftOnlyNode (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ x ‚àà A :: Œì, LeftDivisionOnly x

def LeftFocusedTree : Nat ‚Üí List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | 0, _, _ => False
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.atom s =>
      LeftOnlyNode Œì (Tp.atom s) ‚àß
        (Œì = [Tp.atom s] ‚à®
          ‚àÉ c ‚àà candidates Œì,
            match c with
            | Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ =>
                LeftFocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß
                  LeftFocusedTree n (L ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s)
            | Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R =>
                LeftFocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß
                  LeftFocusedTree n (Œì‚ÇÅ ++ [B] ++ R) (Tp.atom s))
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.ldiv A B =>
      LeftOnlyNode Œì (Tp.ldiv A B) ‚àß Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚àß LeftFocusedTree n ([A] ++ Œì) B
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.rdiv B A =>
      LeftOnlyNode Œì (Tp.rdiv B A) ‚àß Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚àß LeftFocusedTree n (Œì ++ [A]) B
op_000062.proof_text:
intro Œì A h
have hbridge : ‚àÄ {n : Nat} {Œì : List Tp} {A : Tp}, LeftOnlyNode Œì A ‚Üí FocusedTree n Œì A ‚Üí LeftFocusedTree n Œì A := by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      intro Œì A hLeft hTree
      cases hTree
  | succ n ih =>
      intro Œì A hLeft hTree
      cases A with
      | atom s =>
          refine ‚ü®hLeft, ?_‚ü©
          rcases hTree with rfl | ‚ü®c, hc, hcTree‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact Or.inl rfl
          ¬∑ rcases c with ‚ü®L, B, A, Œî, Œõ‚ü© | ‚ü®Œì‚ÇÅ, Œî, A, B, R‚ü©
            ¬∑ exfalso
              have hEq : L ++ [B ‚ß∏ A] ++ Œî ++ Œõ = Œì := by
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Œì := Œì) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ) hc)
              have hBad : LeftDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := by
                apply hLeft
                rw [‚Üê hEq]
                simp
              simp [LeftDivisionOnly] at hBad
            ¬∑ right
              refine ‚ü®Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R, hc, ?_‚ü©
              have hEq : Œì‚ÇÅ ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ R = Œì := by
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Œì := Œì) (c := Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R) hc)
              have hAB : LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B := by
                have : LeftDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B) := by
                  apply hLeft
                  rw [‚Üê hEq]
                  simp
                simpa [LeftDivisionOnly] using this
              have hŒî : LeftOnlyNode Œî A := by
                intro x hx
                rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
                ¬∑ exact hAB.1
                ¬∑ apply hLeft
                  rw [‚Üê hEq]
                  simp [hx]
              have hMain : LeftOnlyNode (Œì‚ÇÅ ++ [B] ++ R) (Tp.atom s) := by
                intro x hx
                rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
                ¬∑ exact hLeft _ (by simp)
                ¬∑ have hx' : x ‚àà (Œì‚ÇÅ ++ [B]) ++ R := by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hx
                  rcases List.mem_append.mp hx' with hxŒì‚ÇÅB | hxR
                  ¬∑ rcases List.mem_append.mp hxŒì‚ÇÅB with hxŒì‚ÇÅ | hxB
                    ¬∑ exact hLeft x (by rw [‚Üê hEq]; simp [hxŒì‚ÇÅ])
                    ¬∑ rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hxB with rfl
                      exact hAB.2
                  ¬∑ exact hLeft x (by rw [‚Üê hEq]; simp [hxR])
              have hcTree' :
                  FocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß FocusedTree n (Œì‚ÇÅ ++ [B] ++ R) (Tp.atom s) := by
                simpa using hcTree
              exact ‚ü®ih hŒî hcTree'.1, ih hMain hcTree'.2‚ü©
      | ldiv A B =>
          rcases hTree with ‚ü®hne, hsub‚ü©
          have hAB : LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B := by
            have : LeftDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B) := hLeft _ (by simp)
            simpa [LeftDivisionOnly] using this
          have hSubLeft : LeftOnlyNode ([A] ++ Œì) B := by
            intro x hx
            rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
            ¬∑ exact hAB.2
            ¬∑ simp at hx
              rcases hx with rfl | hx
              ¬∑ exact hAB.1
              ¬∑ exact hLeft x (by simp [hx])
          exact ‚ü®hLeft, hne, ih hSubLeft hsub‚ü©
      | rdiv B A =>
          have hBad : LeftDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := hLeft _ (by simp)
          simp [LeftDivisionOnly] at hBad
rcases (thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023 (Œì := Œì) (A := A)).1 h.2 with ‚ü®n, hn‚ü©
exact ‚ü®n, hbridge h.1 hn‚ü©

op_000053.source_statement: For every nonempty context Œì and types A, B, LeftRep Œì (A ‚ßπ B) if and only if LeftRep (A :: Œì) B.
op_000053.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A B : Tp), Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚Üí (LeftRep Œì (A ‚ßπ B) ‚Üî LeftRep (A :: Œì) B)
op_000053.statement_formalization_notes: Interpreted 'nonempty context' as the assumption Œì ‚âÝ [].

op_000053.proof_sketch: Rewrite both sides with `thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058`. The left-only side is equivalent because `LeftDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B)` unfolds to `LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B`, so `(A ‚ßπ B) :: Œì` and `B :: A :: Œì` carry the same left-only information. For derivability, use `ldiv_invertible` in one direction and `Sequent.ldiv_r` with `Œì ‚âÝ []` in the other.
op_000053.proof_text:
intro Œì A B hne
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  have hExact := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 Œì (A ‚ßπ B)).mp h
  have hAB : LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B := by
    simpa [LeftDivisionOnly] using (hExact.1 (A ‚ßπ B) (by simp))
  have hLeft : ‚àÄ x ‚àà B :: A :: Œì, LeftDivisionOnly x := by
    intro x hx
    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
    ¬∑ exact hAB.2
    ¬∑ rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
      ¬∑ exact hAB.1
      ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hx])
  have hSeq : (A :: Œì) ‚áí B := by
    simpa using (ldiv_invertible (Œì := Œì) (A := A) (B := B) hExact.2)
  exact (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 (A :: Œì) B).2 ‚ü®hLeft, hSeq‚ü©
¬∑ intro h
  have hExact := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 (A :: Œì) B).mp h
  have hLeft : ‚àÄ x ‚àà (A ‚ßπ B) :: Œì, LeftDivisionOnly x := by
    intro x hx
    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
    ¬∑ have hAB : LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B := by
        exact ‚ü®hExact.1 A (by simp), hExact.1 B (by simp)‚ü©
      simpa [LeftDivisionOnly] using hAB
    ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hx])
  have hSeq : Œì ‚áí (A ‚ßπ B) := by
    simpa using (Sequent.ldiv_r (Œì := Œì) (A := A) (B := B) hne hExact.2)
  exact (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 Œì (A ‚ßπ B)).2 ‚ü®hLeft, hSeq‚ü©

op_000067.source_statement: For every context Œì and type A, if every formula in A :: Œì is RightDivisionOnly and Œì ‚áí A, then there exists a focused proof tree for Œì ‚áí A such that every node Œî ‚áí B in the tree also satisfies ‚àÄ x ‚àà B :: Œî, RightDivisionOnly x.
op_000067.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), ((RightOnlyNode Œì A) ‚àß Œì ‚áí A) ‚Üí ‚àÉ n : Nat, RightFocusedTree n Œì A
op_000067.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized the nodewise condition via the right-oriented mirror of `LeftOnlyNode` and `LeftFocusedTree`, matching the existing left-focused witness pattern in `Derived.lean`.

op_000067.proof_sketch: Define the right-handed mirrors `RightOnlyNode` and `RightFocusedTree`, then reuse `thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023` and upgrade any `FocusedTree` to a `RightFocusedTree` by induction on the height, exactly mirroring the existing left-only theorem with the `Cand.ldiv` and `Cand.rdiv` roles swapped.
op_000067.prelude_code:
abbrev RightOnlyNode (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp) : Prop :=
  ‚àÄ x ‚àà A :: Œì, RightDivisionOnly x

def RightFocusedTree : Nat ‚Üí List Tp ‚Üí Tp ‚Üí Prop
  | 0, _, _ => False
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.atom s =>
      RightOnlyNode Œì (Tp.atom s) ‚àß
        (Œì = [Tp.atom s] ‚à®
          ‚àÉ c ‚àà candidates Œì,
            match c with
            | Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ =>
                RightFocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß
                  RightFocusedTree n (L ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s)
            | Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R =>
                RightFocusedTree n Œî A ‚àß
                  RightFocusedTree n (Œì‚ÇÅ ++ [B] ++ R) (Tp.atom s))
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.ldiv A B =>
      RightOnlyNode Œì (Tp.ldiv A B) ‚àß Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚àß RightFocusedTree n ([A] ++ Œì) B
  | n + 1, Œì, Tp.rdiv B A =>
      RightOnlyNode Œì (Tp.rdiv B A) ‚àß Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚àß RightFocusedTree n (Œì ++ [A]) B
op_000067.proof_text:
intro Œì A h
have hbridge : ‚àÄ {n : Nat} {Œì : List Tp} {A : Tp}, RightOnlyNode Œì A ‚Üí FocusedTree n Œì A ‚Üí RightFocusedTree n Œì A := by
  intro n
  induction n with
  | zero =>
      intro Œì A hRight hTree
      cases hTree
  | succ n ih =>
      intro Œì A hRight hTree
      cases A with
      | atom s =>
          refine ‚ü®hRight, ?_‚ü©
          rcases hTree with rfl | ‚ü®c, hc, hcTree‚ü©
          ¬∑ exact Or.inl rfl
          ¬∑ rcases c with ‚ü®L, B, A, Œî, Œõ‚ü© | ‚ü®Œì‚ÇÅ, Œî, A, B, R‚ü©
            ¬∑ right
              refine ‚ü®Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ, hc, ?_‚ü©
              have hEq : L ++ [B ‚ß∏ A] ++ Œî ++ Œõ = Œì := by
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Œì := Œì) (c := Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ) hc)
              have hBA : RightDivisionOnly B ‚àß RightDivisionOnly A := by
                have : RightDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := by
                  apply hRight
                  rw [‚Üê hEq]
                  simp
                simpa [RightDivisionOnly] using this
              have hŒî : RightOnlyNode Œî A := by
                intro x hx
                rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
                ¬∑ exact hBA.2
                ¬∑ apply hRight
                  rw [‚Üê hEq]
                  simp [hx]
              have hMain : RightOnlyNode (L ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s) := by
                intro x hx
                rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
                ¬∑ exact hRight _ (by simp)
                ¬∑ have hx' : x ‚àà (L ++ [B]) ++ Œõ := by
                    simpa [List.append_assoc] using hx
                  rcases List.mem_append.mp hx' with hxLB | hxŒõ
                  ¬∑ rcases List.mem_append.mp hxLB with hxL | hxB
                    ¬∑ exact hRight x (by rw [‚Üê hEq]; simp [hxL])
                    ¬∑ rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hxB with rfl
                      exact hBA.1
                  ¬∑ exact hRight x (by rw [‚Üê hEq]; simp [hxŒõ])
              exact ‚ü®ih hŒî hcTree.1, ih hMain hcTree.2‚ü©
            ¬∑ exfalso
              have hEq : Œì‚ÇÅ ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ R = Œì := by
                simpa using (candidates_list_degree (Œì := Œì) (c := Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R) hc)
              have hBad : RightDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B) := by
                apply hRight
                rw [‚Üê hEq]
                simp
              simp [RightDivisionOnly] at hBad
      | ldiv A B =>
          have hBad : RightDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B) := hRight _ (by simp)
          simp [RightDivisionOnly] at hBad
      | rdiv B A =>
          rcases hTree with ‚ü®hne, hsub‚ü©
          have hBA : RightDivisionOnly B ‚àß RightDivisionOnly A := by
            have : RightDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := hRight _ (by simp)
            simpa [RightDivisionOnly] using this
          have hSubRight : RightOnlyNode (Œì ++ [A]) B := by
            intro x hx
            rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
            ¬∑ exact hBA.1
            ¬∑ have hx' : x ‚àà Œì ‚à® x = A := by
                simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton] using hx
              rcases hx' with hxŒì | rfl
              ¬∑ exact hRight x (by simp [hxŒì])
              ¬∑ exact hBA.2
          exact ‚ü®hRight, hne, ih hSubRight hsub‚ü©
rcases (thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023 (Œì := Œì) (A := A)).1 h.2 with ‚ü®n, hn‚ü©
exact ‚ü®n, hbridge h.1 hn‚ü©

op_000054.source_statement: For every nonempty context Œì and types A, B, RightRep Œì (B ‚ß∏ A) if and only if RightRep (Œì ++ [A]) B.
op_000054.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A B : Tp), Œì ‚âÝ [] ‚Üí (RightRep Œì (B ‚ß∏ A) ‚Üî RightRep (Œì ++ [A]) B)
op_000054.statement_formalization_notes: Interpreted 'nonempty context' as the assumption Œì ‚âÝ [], mirroring the solved left-fragment inversion statement.

op_000054.proof_sketch: Rewrite both `RightRep` propositions using `thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059`. The forward direction uses right-only decomposition of `B ‚ß∏ A` plus `rdiv_invertible`; the reverse direction uses right-only data from `RightRep (Œì ++ [A]) B` plus `Sequent.rdiv_r` and the nonemptiness hypothesis.
op_000054.proof_text:
intro Œì A B hne
constructor
¬∑ intro hRight
  have hExact := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 Œì (B ‚ß∏ A)).mp hRight
  have hBA : RightDivisionOnly B ‚àß RightDivisionOnly A := by
    have hDiv : RightDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := hExact.1 _ (by simp)
    simpa [RightDivisionOnly] using hDiv
  apply (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 (Œì ++ [A]) B).2
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro x hx
    have hx' : x = B ‚à® x ‚àà Œì ‚à® x = A := by
      simpa [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, or_assoc, or_left_comm, or_comm] using hx
    rcases hx' with rfl | hxŒì | rfl
    ¬∑ exact hBA.1
    ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hxŒì])
    ¬∑ exact hBA.2
  ¬∑ exact rdiv_invertible hExact.2
¬∑ intro hRight
  have hExact := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 (Œì ++ [A]) B).mp hRight
  have hB : RightDivisionOnly B := hExact.1 _ (by simp)
  have hA : RightDivisionOnly A := hExact.1 _ (by simp)
  apply (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 Œì (B ‚ß∏ A)).2
  constructor
  ¬∑ intro x hx
    rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hxŒì
    ¬∑ exact ‚ü®hB, hA‚ü©
    ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hxŒì])
  ¬∑ exact Sequent.rdiv_r hne hExact.2

op_000065.source_statement: For every context Œì and atom s, HasRightOrientedDerivation Œì (Tp.atom s) if and only if there exist n ‚â• 0, types A‚ÇÅ, ..., A‚Çô, and blocks Œî‚ÇÅ, ..., Œî‚Çô such that Œì = [(((Tp.atom s ‚ß∏ A‚Çô) ‚ß∏ ...) ‚ß∏ A‚ÇÅ)] ++ Œî‚ÇÅ ++ ... ++ Œî‚Çô and HasRightOrientedDerivation Œî·µ¢ A·µ¢ for each i.
op_000065.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (s : String), HasRightOrientedDerivation Œì (Tp.atom s) ‚Üî ‚àÉ As : List Tp, ‚àÉ Œîs : List (List Tp), Œì = [RightAtomicHead s As] ++ List.flatten Œîs ‚àß List.Forall‚ÇÇ (fun Œî A => HasRightOrientedDerivation Œî A) Œîs As
op_000065.statement_formalization_notes: Lists encode the finite witnesses: `As = [A‚ÇÅ, ..., A‚Çô]` and `Œîs = [Œî‚ÇÅ, ..., Œî‚Çô]`. The helper `RightAtomicHead s As` denotes the nested type `(((Tp.atom s ‚ß∏ A‚Çô) ‚ß∏ ...) ‚ß∏ A‚ÇÅ)`, and `List.Forall‚ÇÇ` pairs each block `Œî·µ¢` with its corresponding type `A·µ¢`.

op_000065.proof_sketch: A different route through `thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059` and `thm_right_only_focused_tree_000067` still leaves the same essential gap: from an atomic right-only branch `Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ`, one must prove that the surviving main subderivation `HasRightOrientedDerivation (L ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (# s)` forces `L = []`, so the principal formula becomes the unique leftmost iterated head. That prefix-elimination step is effectively the missing right-fragment inversion bridge behind open problem `op_000054`. I also checked small right-fragment examples computationally and found no counterexample, so `counterexample` is not justified; but I do not have a short defensible Lean proof of the required stronger lemma from the current verified library alone.

op_000075.source_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), (‚àÉ n : Nat, RightFocusedTree n Œì A) ‚Üî ((RightOnlyNode Œì A) ‚àß Œì ‚áí A)
op_000075.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), (‚àÉ n : Nat, RightFocusedTree n Œì A) ‚Üî ((RightOnlyNode Œì A) ‚àß Œì ‚áí A)
op_000075.statement_formalization_notes: The input statement is already in repository notation and matches the existing right-only existence theorem upgraded to an iff.

op_000075.proof_sketch: Use the existing theorem for the `(RightOnlyNode Œì A ‚àß Œì ‚áí A) ‚Üí ‚àÉ n, RightFocusedTree n Œì A` direction. For the converse, induct on a witness `RightFocusedTree n Œì A` to forget the right-only annotations and obtain `FocusedTree n Œì A`; the root constructor of `RightFocusedTree` simultaneously exposes `RightOnlyNode Œì A`. Then apply `thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023` to recover `Œì ‚áí A`.
op_000075.proof_text:
intro Œì A
constructor
¬∑ rintro ‚ü®n, htree‚ü©
  have hforget : ‚àÄ {n : Nat} {Œì : List Tp} {A : Tp}, RightFocusedTree n Œì A ‚Üí FocusedTree n Œì A := by
    intro n
    induction n with
    | zero =>
        intro Œì A h
        cases h
    | succ n ih =>
        intro Œì A h
        cases A with
        | atom s =>
            rcases h with ‚ü®_, h'‚ü©
            rcases h' with rfl | ‚ü®c, hc, hcTree‚ü©
            ¬∑ exact Or.inl rfl
            ¬∑ rcases c with ‚ü®L, B, A, Œî, Œõ‚ü© | ‚ü®Œì‚ÇÅ, Œî, A, B, R‚ü©
              ¬∑ right
                refine ‚ü®Cand.rdiv L B A Œî Œõ, hc, ?_‚ü©
                exact ‚ü®ih hcTree.1, ih hcTree.2‚ü©
              ¬∑ right
                refine ‚ü®Cand.ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R, hc, ?_‚ü©
                exact ‚ü®ih hcTree.1, ih hcTree.2‚ü©
        | ldiv A B =>
            rcases h with ‚ü®_, hne, hsub‚ü©
            exact ‚ü®hne, ih hsub‚ü©
        | rdiv B A =>
            rcases h with ‚ü®_, hne, hsub‚ü©
            exact ‚ü®hne, ih hsub‚ü©
  have hright : RightOnlyNode Œì A := by
    cases n with
    | zero =>
        cases htree
    | succ n =>
        cases A with
        | atom s => exact htree.1
        | ldiv A B => exact htree.1
        | rdiv B A => exact htree.1
  refine ‚ü®hright, ?_‚ü©
  exact (thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023 (Œì := Œì) (A := A)).2 ‚ü®n, hforget htree‚ü©
¬∑ intro h
  exact thm_right_only_focused_tree_000067 Œì A h

op_000071.source_statement: `‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), (‚àÉ n : Nat, LeftFocusedTree n Œì A) ‚Üî ((LeftOnlyNode Œì A) ‚àß Œì ‚áí A)`
op_000071.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), (‚àÉ n : Nat, LeftFocusedTree n Œì A) ‚Üî ((LeftOnlyNode Œì A) ‚àß Œì ‚áí A)
op_000071.statement_formalization_notes: Used the existing `LeftOnlyNode` and `LeftFocusedTree` abbreviations from `Derived.lean` and kept the repository's sequent notation.

op_000071.proof_sketch: For the forward direction, induct on the height witness and forget the `LeftOnlyNode` annotations to obtain a plain `FocusedTree`; the root annotation already gives `LeftOnlyNode Œì A`, and `thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023` converts the forgotten tree into `Œì ‚áí A`. The reverse direction is exactly `thm_left_only_focused_tree_000062`.
op_000071.proof_text:
intro Œì A
constructor
¬∑ rintro ‚ü®n, hTree‚ü©
  have hForget :
      ‚àÄ {n : Nat} {Œì : List Tp} {A : Tp},
        LeftFocusedTree n Œì A ‚Üí LeftOnlyNode Œì A ‚àß FocusedTree n Œì A := by
    intro n
    induction n with
    | zero =>
        intro Œì A h
        cases h
    | succ n ih =>
        intro Œì A h
        cases A with
        | atom s =>
            rcases h with ‚ü®hLeft, hTree‚ü©
            refine ‚ü®hLeft, ?_‚ü©
            rcases hTree with rfl | ‚ü®c, hc, hcTree‚ü©
            ¬∑ exact Or.inl rfl
            ¬∑ refine Or.inr ‚ü®c, hc, ?_‚ü©
              cases c with
              | rdiv L B A Œî Œõ =>
                  rcases hcTree with ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
                  exact ‚ü®(ih h1).2, (ih h2).2‚ü©
              | ldiv Œì‚ÇÅ Œî A B R =>
                  rcases hcTree with ‚ü®h1, h2‚ü©
                  exact ‚ü®(ih h1).2, (ih h2).2‚ü©
        | ldiv A B =>
            rcases h with ‚ü®hLeft, hNe, hSub‚ü©
            exact ‚ü®hLeft, ‚ü®hNe, (ih hSub).2‚ü©‚ü©
        | rdiv B A =>
            rcases h with ‚ü®hLeft, hNe, hSub‚ü©
            exact ‚ü®hLeft, ‚ü®hNe, (ih hSub).2‚ü©‚ü©
  have hRoot := hForget hTree
  exact ‚ü®hRoot.1, (thm_sequent_iff_focused_tree_000023 (Œì := Œì) (A := A)).2 ‚ü®n, hRoot.2‚ü©‚ü©
¬∑ intro h
  exact thm_left_only_focused_tree_000062 Œì A h

op_000078.source_statement: For every context Œì and atom s, RightRep Œì (Tp.atom s) iff either Œì = [Tp.atom s] or there exist contexts ŒÝ, Œî, Œõ and types A, B such that Œì = ŒÝ ++ [B ‚ß∏ A] ++ Œî ++ Œõ, RightRep Œî A, and RightRep (ŒÝ ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s).
op_000078.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (s : String), RightRep Œì (# s) ‚Üî (Œì = [# s] ‚à® ‚àÉ (pi delta lam : List Tp) (A B : Tp), Œì = pi ++ [B ‚ß∏ A] ++ delta ++ lam ‚àß RightRep delta A ‚àß RightRep (pi ++ [B] ++ lam) (# s))
op_000078.statement_formalization_notes: Minimal repair of the previous formalization: replaced the binder name `ŒÝ` with ASCII `pi`, since `ŒÝ` is parsed specially by Lean and is not accepted here as a local identifier.

op_000078.proof_sketch: Use exactness of right-oriented derivations to turn `RightRep` into a right-only derivable sequent. Decompose an atomic sequent with `thm_atom_sequent_decompose_000009`; the `ldiv` branch contradicts right-only orientation, while the `rdiv` branch restricts orientation to the two premises, yielding the required recursive `RightRep` facts. Conversely, combine the two recursive `RightRep` hypotheses via `Sequent.rdiv_l` and rebuild `RightRep` using uniform right-orientation.
op_000078.proof_text:
intro Œì s
constructor
¬∑ intro hRight
  have hExact := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 Œì (# s)).mp hRight
  rcases thm_atom_sequent_decompose_000009 (Œì := Œì) (s := s) hExact.2 with hAx | hCases
  ¬∑ exact Or.inl hAx
  ¬∑ rcases hCases with ‚ü®pi, B, A, delta, lam, hŒì, hDeltaSeq, hMainSeq‚ü© | ‚ü®pi, delta, A, B, lam, hŒì, hDeltaSeq, hMainSeq‚ü©
    ¬∑ have hBA : RightDivisionOnly B ‚àß RightDivisionOnly A := by
        have hDiv : RightDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := by
          exact hExact.1 _ (by simp [hŒì])
        simpa [RightDivisionOnly] using hDiv
      have hDeltaRight : ‚àÄ x ‚àà A :: delta, RightDivisionOnly x := by
        intro x hx
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hxDelta
        ¬∑ exact hBA.2
        ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hŒì, hxDelta])
      have hMainRight : ‚àÄ x ‚àà (# s) :: (pi ++ [B] ++ lam), RightDivisionOnly x := by
        intro x hx
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hxMain
        ¬∑ exact hExact.1 _ (by simp)
        ¬∑ have hxMain' : x ‚àà pi ‚à® x = B ‚à® x ‚àà lam := by
            simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton, or_assoc] using hxMain
          rcases hxMain' with hxPi | rfl | hxLam
          ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hŒì, hxPi])
          ¬∑ exact hBA.1
          ¬∑ exact hExact.1 x (by simp [hŒì, hxLam])
      refine Or.inr ‚ü®pi, delta, lam, A, B, hŒì, ?_, ?_‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 delta A).2 ‚ü®hDeltaRight, hDeltaSeq‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 (pi ++ [B] ++ lam) (# s)).2 ‚ü®hMainRight, hMainSeq‚ü©
    ¬∑ have hBad : RightDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B) := by
        exact hExact.1 _ (by simp [hŒì])
      simp [RightDivisionOnly] at hBad
¬∑ rintro (rfl | ‚ü®pi, delta, lam, A, B, hŒì, hDeltaRight, hMainRight‚ü©)
  ¬∑ refine (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 [# s] (# s)).2 ?_
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro x hx
      rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
      ¬∑ simp [RightDivisionOnly]
      ¬∑ rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hx with rfl
        simp [RightDivisionOnly]
    ¬∑ exact Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.ax
  ¬∑ subst hŒì
    have hDeltaExact := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 delta A).mp hDeltaRight
    have hMainExact := (thm_right_oriented_derivation_exact_000059 (pi ++ [B] ++ lam) (# s)).mp hMainRight
    refine (thm_uniform_orientation_implies_oriented_derivation_000055 (pi ++ [B ‚ß∏ A] ++ delta ++ lam) (# s)).2 ?_
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro x hx
      have hx' : x = # s ‚à® x ‚àà pi ‚à® x = B ‚ß∏ A ‚à® x ‚àà delta ‚à® x ‚àà lam := by
        simpa [List.mem_cons, List.mem_append, List.mem_singleton, or_assoc, or_left_comm, or_comm] using hx
      rcases hx' with rfl | hxPi | rfl | hxDelta | hxLam
      ¬∑ simp [RightDivisionOnly]
      ¬∑ exact hMainExact.1 x (by simp [hxPi])
      ¬∑ exact ‚ü®hMainExact.1 B (by simp), hDeltaExact.1 A (by simp)‚ü©
      ¬∑ exact hDeltaExact.1 x (by simp [hxDelta])
      ¬∑ exact hMainExact.1 x (by simp [hxLam])
    ¬∑ simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Sequent.rdiv_l
          (Œî := delta) (A := A) (Œì := pi) (B := B) (Œõ := lam) (C := # s)
          hDeltaExact.2 hMainExact.2)

op_000072.source_statement: For every context Œì and atom s, LeftRep Œì (Tp.atom s) iff either Œì = [Tp.atom s] or there exist contexts ŒÝ, Œî, Œõ and types A, B such that Œì = ŒÝ ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ Œõ, LeftRep Œî A, and LeftRep (ŒÝ ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s).
op_000072.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (s : String), LeftRep Œì (Tp.atom s) ‚Üî (Œì = [Tp.atom s] ‚à® ‚àÉ Pi : List Tp, ‚àÉ Œî : List Tp, ‚àÉ Œõ : List Tp, ‚àÉ A : Tp, ‚àÉ B : Tp, Œì = Pi ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ Œõ ‚àß LeftRep Œî A ‚àß LeftRep (Pi ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s))
op_000072.statement_formalization_notes: Repaired the parser failure by replacing the existential context name `ŒÝ` with ASCII `Pi` and by using fully nested existential binders, which is the more conservative Lean syntax for this witness package.

op_000072.proof_sketch: Use `thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058` to convert `LeftRep` into left-only derivability. Apply `thm_atom_sequent_decompose_000009`; the `rdiv` branch contradicts left-onlyness, while the `ldiv` branch gives the required recursive decomposition and both recursive `LeftRep` facts via exactness again. Conversely, from the recursive witnesses recover left-only derivations for the premises, combine them with `Sequent.ldiv_l`, verify the rebuilt sequent is still left-only, and convert back to `LeftRep`.
op_000072.proof_text:
intro Œì s
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  have hExact := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 Œì (Tp.atom s)).mp h
  rcases thm_atom_sequent_decompose_000009 hExact.2 with hAx | hRest
  ¬∑ exact Or.inl hAx
  ¬∑ rcases hRest with ‚ü®Pi, B, A, Œî, Œõ, hEq, hŒîSeq, hMainSeq‚ü© | ‚ü®Pi, Œî, A, B, Œõ, hEq, hŒîSeq, hMainSeq‚ü©
    ¬∑ exfalso
      have hBad : LeftDivisionOnly (B ‚ß∏ A) := by
        apply hExact.1
        rw [hEq]
        simp
      cases hBad
    ¬∑ have hAB : LeftDivisionOnly A ‚àß LeftDivisionOnly B := by
        have hSlash : LeftDivisionOnly (A ‚ßπ B) := by
          apply hExact.1
          rw [hEq]
          simp
        simpa [LeftDivisionOnly] using hSlash
      have hŒîLeft : ‚àÄ x ‚àà A :: Œî, LeftDivisionOnly x := by
        intro x hx
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
        ¬∑ exact hAB.1
        ¬∑ apply hExact.1
          rw [hEq]
          simp [hx]
      have hMainLeft : ‚àÄ x ‚àà Tp.atom s :: (Pi ++ [B] ++ Œõ), LeftDivisionOnly x := by
        intro x hx
        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
        ¬∑ exact hExact.1 _ (by simp)
        ¬∑ have hx' : x ‚àà (Pi ++ [B]) ++ Œõ := by simpa [List.append_assoc] using hx
          rcases List.mem_append.mp hx' with hxPiB | hxŒõ
          ¬∑ rcases List.mem_append.mp hxPiB with hxPi | hxB
            ¬∑ exact hExact.1 _ (by rw [hEq]; simp [hxPi])
            ¬∑ rcases List.mem_singleton.mp hxB with rfl
              exact hAB.2
          ¬∑ exact hExact.1 _ (by rw [hEq]; simp [hxŒõ])
      exact Or.inr ‚ü®Pi, Œî, Œõ, A, B, hEq, (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 Œî A).2 ‚ü®hŒîLeft, hŒîSeq‚ü©, (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 (Pi ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s)).2 ‚ü®hMainLeft, hMainSeq‚ü©‚ü©
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with rfl | ‚ü®Pi, Œî, Œõ, A, B, hEq, hŒî, hMain‚ü©
  ¬∑ apply (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 [Tp.atom s] (Tp.atom s)).2
    constructor
    ¬∑ intro x hx
      simp at hx
      subst hx
      simp [LeftDivisionOnly]
    ¬∑ exact Sequent.ax
  ¬∑ have hŒîExact := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 Œî A).mp hŒî
    have hMainExact := (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 (Pi ++ [B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s)).mp hMain
    have hLeft : ‚àÄ x ‚àà Tp.atom s :: (Pi ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ Œõ), LeftDivisionOnly x := by
      intro x hx
      rcases List.mem_cons.mp hx with rfl | hx
      ¬∑ exact hMainExact.1 _ (by simp)
      ¬∑ have hx' : x ‚àà Pi ‚à® x ‚àà Œî ‚à® x = A ‚ßπ B ‚à® x ‚àà Œõ := by
          simpa [List.mem_append, List.mem_cons, or_assoc, or_left_comm, or_comm] using hx
        rcases hx' with hxPi | hxŒî | rfl | hxŒõ
        ¬∑ exact hMainExact.1 _ (by simp [hxPi])
        ¬∑ exact hŒîExact.1 _ (by simp [hxŒî])
        ¬∑ have hA : LeftDivisionOnly A := hŒîExact.1 _ (by simp)
          have hB : LeftDivisionOnly B := hMainExact.1 _ (by simp)
          simpa [LeftDivisionOnly] using And.intro hA hB
        ¬∑ exact hMainExact.1 _ (by simp [hxŒõ])
    have hSeq : Pi ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ Œõ ‚áí Tp.atom s := by
      simpa [List.append_assoc] using
        (Sequent.ldiv_l (Œî := Œî) (A := A) (Œì := Pi) (B := B) (Œõ := Œõ) (C := Tp.atom s) hŒîExact.2 hMainExact.2)
    simpa [hEq] using (thm_left_oriented_derivation_exact_000058 (Pi ++ Œî ++ [A ‚ßπ B] ++ Œõ) (Tp.atom s)).2 ‚ü®hLeft, hSeq‚ü©

op_000080.source_statement: For every context Œì and type A, RightRep Œì A iff there exist a context ŒìR and a type AR in the pure right fragment such that Œì and A are exactly the translations of ŒìR and AR into the full product-free language and ŒìR ‚áí AR in the pure right calculus.
op_000080.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), RightRep Œì A ‚Üî ‚àÉ ŒìR : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Tp, ‚àÉ AR : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Tp, Œì = ŒìR.map Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Tp.toProductFree ‚àß A = AR.toProductFree ‚àß Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.Sequent ŒìR AR
op_000080.statement_formalization_notes: Formalized as the direct right-fragment mirror of open problem op_000086, using the repository‚Äôs explicit translation style `map ...toProductFree` and `Right.Sequent`.

op_000080.proof_sketch: `RightRep` is already defined by existence of right-fragment witnesses whose `ctxToProductFree`/`toProductFree` images are the ambient context and type. Since `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.ctxToProductFree` is definitionally `List.map Tp.toProductFree`, both directions are immediate by reusing the same witnesses and `simpa` after symmetry of the equalities.
op_000080.proof_text:
intro Œì A
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with ‚ü®ŒìR, AR, hŒì, hA, hseq‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®ŒìR, AR, ?_, ?_, hseq‚ü©
  ¬∑ simpa [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.ctxToProductFree] using hŒì.symm
  ¬∑ simpa using hA.symm
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with ‚ü®ŒìR, AR, hŒì, hA, hseq‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®ŒìR, AR, ?_, ?_, hseq‚ü©
  ¬∑ simpa [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Right.ctxToProductFree] using hŒì.symm
  ¬∑ simpa using hA.symm

op_000086.source_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), LeftRep Œì A ‚Üî ‚àÉ ŒìL : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp, ‚àÉ AL : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp, Œì = ŒìL.map Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.toProductFree ‚àß A = AL.toProductFree ‚àß Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent ŒìL AL
op_000086.formalized_statement: ‚àÄ (Œì : List Tp) (A : Tp), LeftRep Œì A ‚Üî ‚àÉ ŒìL : List Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp, ‚àÉ AL : Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp, Œì = ŒìL.map Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Tp.toProductFree ‚àß A = AL.toProductFree ‚àß Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.Sequent ŒìL AL
op_000086.statement_formalization_notes: Used root ProductFree `Tp` and `LeftRep`, with explicit witnesses in `Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left` embedded via `toProductFree`.

op_000086.proof_sketch: Unfold `LeftRep`, reuse the witnesses, and rewrite `Left.ctxToProductFree` to `List.map Left.Tp.toProductFree`; both directions are identical up to symmetry of the displayed equalities.
op_000086.proof_text:
intro Œì A
constructor
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with ‚ü®ŒìL, AL, hŒì, hA, hseq‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®ŒìL, AL, ?_, ?_, hseq‚ü©
  ¬∑ simpa [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.ctxToProductFree] using hŒì.symm
  ¬∑ simpa using hA.symm
¬∑ intro h
  rcases h with ‚ü®ŒìL, AL, hŒì, hA, hseq‚ü©
  refine ‚ü®ŒìL, AL, ?_, ?_, hseq‚ü©
  ¬∑ simpa [Mathling.Lambek.ProductFree.Left.ctxToProductFree] using hŒì.symm
  ¬∑ simpa using hA.symm
